plan.lockedAnomalies: Anomaly detection requires a Developer plan or higher.

products.heroTitle: Tools built for space weather intelligence.
products.heroSub: Products for monitoring, predicting, and integrating space weather. From live dashboards to developer APIs and automated alerts.
products.d1Desc: Monitor space weather in real time. Track geomagnetic activity, solar wind, X-ray flux, near-Earth objects, ISS position, aurora forecasts, and ML-powered storm predictions — all from a single interface, updated continuously from NOAA, NASA, and global observatories.
products.d2Desc: Integrate real-time space weather data into your applications. Low-latency REST endpoints for Kp index, solar wind, X-ray flux, and anomaly alerts — plus a predictive layer with 3-hour ML forecasts and confidence intervals.
products.d3Desc: Get alerted the moment space weather conditions change. Know when storms intensify, custom thresholds are exceeded, or satellite conditions shift — delivered via webhook or email the instant it happens.

about.comingSoonSub: Our story and mission are coming soon.

docs.comingSoonSub: API documentation and integration guides are coming soon.

blog.comingSoonSub: Articles and updates on space weather and platform news coming soon.

pricing.title: Simple, transparent pricing.
pricing.sub: Start free. Scale when you're ready. No hidden fees.

landing.heroTitle: See space weather before it impacts Earth.
landing.heroSub: Real-time monitoring of solar activity and geomagnetic storms — before they disrupt satellites, GPS, and communications.
landing.heroStat: 3-hour advance warnings for space weather events affecting Earth.
landing.c1Body: Track geomagnetic activity in real time and understand when conditions are likely to intensify — before they disrupt power grids, communications, or satellite operations.
landing.c2Body: See when and where auroras may appear based on current space weather conditions — down to your latitude.
landing.c3Body: Follow ISS and Starlink constellations with continuously updated orbital data — and anticipate disruptions from geomagnetic storms.
landing.c4Body: Identify sudden changes in solar wind, radiation, and geomagnetic indices — before they impact GPS accuracy, HF radio, or power infrastructure.
landing.liveDesc: Real-time data streams updated continuously from global observatories and space agencies.
landing.p1Body: Anticipate near-term space weather activity using machine learning models trained on decades of historical data.
landing.p2Body: Forecasts include probability ranges to reflect uncertainty in solar activity dynamics.
landing.p3Body: Identify emerging geomagnetic disturbances before they fully develop.
landing.howBody: We combine real-time observational data with predictive modeling from global space weather agencies and observatories.
landing.howMl: A machine learning forecasting system processes time-series space weather data to estimate short-term geomagnetic activity levels.
landing.techNote: API access available for integration into research, monitoring systems, and automation workflows.
landing.sourcesNote: Trusted space and atmospheric data providers
landing.s2: NASA NeoWs, EPIC, and APOD datasets
landing.a1desc: Track aurora visibility and solar storms from your location in real time.
landing.a2desc: Access time-series space weather data and ML forecast outputs for analysis and modeling.
landing.a3desc: Get advance warnings before geomagnetic events affect orbital operations and communications.
landing.a4desc: Integrate live space weather data, anomaly alerts, and Kp forecasts into your apps via REST API.
landing.a5desc: Monitor conditions that affect GPS accuracy, HF radio communications, and power grid stability.
landing.ctaSub: Get real-time insights and predictive intelligence for space weather activity affecting Earth.
landing.ctaBody: Create a free account and start tracking now.
landing.footerNote: Astraeusio · Built on open scientific data · Powered by NOAA, NASA, and open-access space weather APIs · BSL 1.1
landing.techModelVal: LSTM neural network with MC Dropout
landing.techTrainingVal: 20+ years of NOAA Kp historical data
landing.techDetectionVal: Multi-signal anomaly pipeline (Kp, solar wind, X-ray, NEO)
landing.ctaSub: Space weather is live and constantly changing. Sign up free — see current conditions the moment you log in.
